title: Buffer Preparation for OnePot PURE Cell-Free System
author: Konstantinos Ragios
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.728hqhw
materials: Add the materials needed the buffer you want to produce in a beaker. The final concentration of the components for the different buffers is presented in Table 1 and Table 2
The buffers are then stored at 4 o C

steps.title: Table 1
steps.instruction: Table 1 : Buffers for Protein Purification %s %s In Stock Buffer B, Glycerol should consists of 60% of the final volume

steps.title: Table 2
steps.instruction: Table 2: Buffers for Ribosome Purification %s %s In Cusion buffer, Sucrose should consists of 30% of the final volume

steps.title: Add some distilled water and mix...
steps.instruction: Add some distilled water and mix with a magnetic stirrer until all the solid components are dissolved Be careful so the volume at this point does not surpass the desired one, otherwise the components' concentration will be wrong on the final product.

steps.title: Add as much distilled water is...
steps.instruction: Add as much distilled water is needed to reach the final volume of the buffers

steps.title: The buffer must be stored at 4 o C
steps.instruction: The buffer must be stored at 4 o C

steps.title: Because β-mercaptoethanol has a short half-life...
steps.instruction: Because β-mercaptoethanol has a short half-life we need to add it again to the buffer every time before we perform an experiment.
